reasoning: Multiple credible sources and reports covering 2023 and early 2025 indicate that financial inclusion in Nigeria was approximately 74% by late 2023, with the strictly "banked" population (those with bank accounts) hovering around 52% (approx. 60 million banked adults). To reach a 95% banked population before 2025 would mean almost doubling the number of banked adults within a single year, which is practically impossible given historical growth rates and continuing infrastructural/trust barriers. Furthermore, the resolution criteria explicitly state that if CBN data is known to be inaccurate, credible independent sources (like EFInA or the World Bank) will be used. Given that EFInA's comprehensive 2023 survey places the inclusive rate at 74% and banking usage far lower, there is essentially zero chance of legitimately achieving 95% by the end of 2024. Therefore, I'm assigning the minimum threshold probability of 2%.
